items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: WNBA Free Agency Starts Monday; Signings To Begin On Saturday
items.blurb: WNBA Free Agency Starts Monday; Signings To Begin On Saturday — The WNBA announced that the free agency period will begin on Monday, with teams allowed to send qualifying offers and offer core player designations. Negotiations will run Wednesday through Friday, with signings set to begin on Saturday. There are currently over 100 free agents in the league. Monday began with Angel Reese getting trad

items.headline: Is Nuggets superstar Nikola Jokic still the best player on the planet?
items.blurb: Is Nuggets superstar Nikola Jokic still the best player on the planet? — Let’s get this out of the way: Oklahoma City Thunder Shai Gilgeous-Alexander should win NBA MVP. He’s putting up 30 a game on absurd efficiency, his Thunder have lapped the field and he’s the most reliable closer in the league.

items.headline: NBA Roundup: Banchero scores 31, Bane adds 25 to help Magic turn back Pistons
items.blurb: NBA Roundup: Banchero scores 31, Bane adds 25 to help Magic turn back Pistons — Paolo Banchero scored 31 points, Desmond Bane added 25 and the Orlando Magic beat the Detroit Pistons 123-107 on Monday night.

items.headline: Record shooting night can't help Grizzlies break losing streak
items.blurb: Record shooting night can't help Grizzlies break losing streak — No NBA team has ever made more three-pointers in a game than the Memphis Grizzlies did Monday night. Unlike the other teams that sank 27 or more threes, the Grizzlies couldn't win the game.

items.blurb: Michigan Holds Off UConn 69-63 To Win NCAA Title — Before Michigan took the floor to face Gonzaga in the title game of the Players Era Championship back in November, Elliot Cadeau made a comment to his teammates. items.blurb: The 'Most 30-point NBA games at 35+' quiz — How many of the NBA players to score at least 30 points five or more times in a season at age 35 or older can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Grizzlies match NBA record by making 29 3-pointers in loss to Cavaliers
items.blurb: Grizzlies match NBA record by making 29 3-pointers in loss to Cavaliers — The Memphis Grizzlies made 29 3-pointers Monday night against Cleveland, matching an NBA single-game record.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham Expected To Return From Collapsed Lung Before Playoffs
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham Expected To Return From Collapsed Lung Before Playoffs — Cade Cunningham was upgraded to doubtful before being ruled out of Monday night's 123-107 loss by the Detroit Pistons to the Orlando Magic. The move signals the Eastern Conference's top seed could have its star player back before the playoffs begin. Cunningham has been sidelined since March 19 with a collapsed left lung and h

items.headline: Hawks' Nickeil Alexander-Walker shows why he's Most Improved Player vs. Knicks
items.blurb: Hawks' Nickeil Alexander-Walker shows why he's Most Improved Player vs. Knicks — Atlanta Hawks guard Nickeil Alexander-Walker inched closer to adding an individual accolade to his resume with a stellar performance in a thriller versus the New York Knicks.

items.headline: Eventful Monday leaves Bulls' Billy Donovan in limbo
items.blurb: Eventful Monday leaves Bulls' Billy Donovan in limbo — Two big events on Monday should have a big effect on Billy Donovan's future. The Chicago Bulls fired Donovan's boss, executive vice president Arturas Karnisovas, and then the University of North Carolina hired Michael Malone as their new head coach.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama Exits Monday's Game With Rib Injury, Award Eligibility At Risk
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama Exits Monday's Game With Rib Injury, Award Eligibility At Risk — The San Antonio Spurs ruled Victor Wembanyama out for the second half of Monday's game against the Philadelphia 76ers due to a left rib contusion. The 22-year-old big man sustained the injury in a collision with 76ers forward Paul George near half-court. The contact occurred with 10:47 remaining in the second quarte

items.headline: McCollum’s game-tying heave from half court doesn’t beat buzzer
items.blurb: McCollum’s game-tying heave from half court doesn’t beat buzzer — Watch as Atlanta Hawks guard CJ McCollum’s game-tying three-point attempt from half court is late by a tenth of a second and doesn’t beat the final buzzer.

items.headline: Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama exits vs. 76ers with rib contusion
items.blurb: Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama exits vs. 76ers with rib contusion — Centre Victor Wembanyama exited Monday’s game against the Philadelphia 76ers after suffering a left rib contusion. The team announced at the start of the second half that he would not return.

items.headline: Hawks’ Alexander-Walker hits pair of clutch threes late vs. Knicks
items.blurb: Hawks’ Alexander-Walker hits pair of clutch threes late vs. Knicks — Watch as Canada’s Nickeil Alexander-Walker hits a pair of clutch threes late to help give the Atlanta Hawks the lead late against the New York Knicks.

items.headline: Bulls close door on frustrating Arturas Karnisovas era
items.blurb: Bulls close door on frustrating Arturas Karnisovas era — One year after hiring Karnisovas, the Chicago Bulls were riding high, first in the Eastern Conference. They never achieved those heights again, and Monday, the Bulls finally pulled the plug.

items.blurb: Time is running out for Magic HC Jamahl Mosley — The Magic have failed to take a step forward this season despite heightened expectations.

items.headline: Insider shares latest on return timelines for Lakers' Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves
items.blurb: Insider shares latest on return timelines for Lakers' Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves — The Los Angeles Lakers hit a brutal stretch last Thursday, and the timing could not be worse. Both Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves went down with injuries during a 139-96 blowout loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder.

items.headline: Michael Malone To Be Next North Carolina Head Coach
items.blurb: Michael Malone To Be Next North Carolina Head Coach — Veteran NBA head coach Michael Malone is headed to the college ranks for his next job. Malone is set to be the next head coach for the University of North Carolina Tar Heels. Before working for ESPN this season, Malone had spent the last 12 seasons as an NBA head coach. He worked for the Sacramento Kings for two years, before spending 10 season

items.blurb: Bulls Fire Arturas Karnisovas, Marc Eversley From Front Office Roles — The Chicago Bulls have fired VP of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas and GM Marc Eversley. Chicago is moving on from Karnisovas after six seasons of leading the team's front office. items.blurb: Latest Cade Cunningham update signals good news for Pistons — After clinching the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference, the Detroit Pistons may be getting their star player back just in time for the postseason.

items.blurb: Bulls make major decision on top executive Arturas Karnisovas — The Bulls have already been eliminated from playoff contention for this season with a 29-49 record.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic: Notches triple-double in OT win
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic: Notches triple-double in OT win — Jokic produced 35 points (15-31 FG, 1-5 3Pt, 4-4 FT), 14 rebounds, 13 assists, five steals and two blocks in 43 minutes during Monday's 137-132 overtime victory over Portland. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Cooper Flagg puts facing LeBron James in perspective after latest matchup
items.blurb: Cooper Flagg puts facing LeBron James in perspective after latest matchup — From the moment he entered the NBA as a teenager, Cooper Flagg has been compared to LeBron James. Now as he's nearing the end of his historic rookie season, those comparisons have only intensified. The rookie reflected on playing against James after Sunday's Mavericks-Lakers matchup.

items.headline: Cameron Payne Out At Least Two Weeks With Hamstring Strain
items.blurb: Cameron Payne Out At Least Two Weeks With Hamstring Strain — Philadelphia 76ers reserve guard Cameron Payne will miss at least two weeks due to a strained hamstring. This injury will keep Payne out through the rest of the regular season, and through the Play-In Tournament. The Sixers currently sit sixth in the Eastern Conference. However, just two games separate the sixth-place 76ers and the Miami

items.headline: Joel Embiid: Strikes for 34 points in loss
items.blurb: Joel Embiid: Strikes for 34 points in loss — Embiid racked up 34 points (8-19 FG, 2-6 3Pt, 16-19 FT), 12 rebounds, one assist and four blocks in 39 minutes during Monday's 115-102 loss to the Spurs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Stephon Castle: Triple-doubles in victory
items.blurb: Stephon Castle: Triple-doubles in victory — Castle contributed 19 points (6-13 FG, 1-2 3Pt, 6-6 FT), 13 assists, 10 rebounds and two steals across 31 minutes during Monday's 115-102 win over the 76ers. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Cooper Flagg Overtakes Kon Knueppel As NBA Rookie Of The Year Favorite After Historic 96-Point Weekend
items.blurb: Cooper Flagg Overtakes Kon Knueppel As NBA Rookie Of The Year Favorite After Historic 96-Point Weekend — Cooper Flagg has surged to the top of NBA Rookie of the Year betting markets after a record-setting two-game stretch. items.headline: Jalen Brunson: Double-doubles in victory
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson: Double-doubles in victory — Brunson recorded 30 points (11-26 FG, 3-8 3Pt, 5-7 FT), 13 assists, three rebounds and two steals across 39 minutes in Monday's 108-105 win over Atlanta. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Paolo Banchero: Drops 31 points in victory
items.blurb: Paolo Banchero: Drops 31 points in victory — Banchero tallied 31 points (10-16 FG, 1-2 3Pt, 10-10 FT), five rebounds, three assists and three steals in 38 minutes during Monday's 123-107 victory over the Pistons. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Rockets’ Thompson puts Payton Jr. on a poster with ferocious and-one slam — Watch as Houston Rockets guard Amen Thompson puts Golden State Warriors guard Gary Payton Jr. on a poster with a ferocious and-one dunk.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo explains why he is hurt by sitting out
items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo explains why he is hurt by sitting out — Giannis Antetokounmpo opened up about his frustration with the Milwaukee Bucks in a new interview, explaining why he is so bothered that the team wants to sit him out for the rest of the season.

items.blurb: Warriors’ Curry blows past Durant and floats in and-one teardrop — Watch as Golden State Warriors guard Steph Curry blows past former teammate Kevin Durant with a hesitation dribble before floating in a beautiful and-one teardrop layup.
